name: agentic_tasks_v1
note: Multi-turn agentic coding tasks for the closed-loop driver (drive.py). Every task requires at least two tool turns, so the SECOND-turn regime -- where users report v7-coder loops ('looped on calling a tool' / 'looped during reasoning on the second attempt' in OpenCode/Kilo) and where the chat-template thinking-reinject path fires -- is always exercised. Tasks are framed around the reported triggers (solar-system-v4, snake game) plus list->write and read->modify->write sequences. Run paired on v7-coder-Q6_K and 128e-Q6_K, same sampler/template, then diff_divergence.py.
system: You are a coding agent operating inside a project directory. You have three tools: list_directory(path), read_file(path), and write_file(path, content). Complete the user's task by calling these tools. Write real, complete code with write_file. When the task is done, reply with a one-line confirmation and stop. Be concise. Do not repeat a tool call you have already made.

tasks.name: list_then_index
tasks.user: First list the project root to see what's there, then create an index.html with a simple 'Hello World' landing page. Confirm when done.

tasks.name: solar_system_v4
tasks.user: Create a 3D solar system simulator. Ensure the planets are correctly positioned, orbit the sun, and are aligned from today's date, with user controls to speed up time. Put everything in a single new file called solar-system-v4.html, then confirm.

tasks.name: snake_game
tasks.user: Write a snake game as a single self-contained HTML file named snake.html. Create the file, then confirm it's done.

tasks.name: read_fix_calc
tasks.user: There's a bug in calc.js: add() returns the wrong result. Read the file, fix the bug, and save it. Confirm when fixed.

tasks.name: read_extend_page
tasks.user: Read site/home.html, add a footer with a copyright line inside the body, and save the updated file. Confirm when done.

tasks.name: explore_then_build

seed_files.README.md:
# My Project
A small web demo.

tasks.user: List the project root, read the README, then build a single-file bouncing-ball animation in ball.html that matches the project theme. Confirm when done.
